Great beach with lots of food options. Not busy during weekdays in early February. We had grilled whole fish and shrimp dish here. Very fresh

Beautiful beach. I went here on a Sunday so it was very crowded. But overall water was nice and kids had fun. We rented a table and ordered food at a restaurant called Super. I didnt do much research before going. Our taxi driver recommended this restaurant. Their food was good, we used the WiFi and bathrooms were available at no extra charge. There were no showers. We paid total 1700 pesos which included 150 tip. We ordered 3 pc fish tacos, shrimp pizza, pop, orange juice, 2 nonalcohol mojitos and pina colada. Mojito was better. Staff was kind and service was great. We had no issues with the restaurant. Only downside is the location which is right in front of the boat docking area. It was super crowded. We kept bumping into people and getting splashed in the face. Thats how crowded it was. I dont swim but my husband went swimming a bit far out and said it was one of the best snorkelling experiences. My 3 year old had a great time as well and my 4 month old slept through the noise. The left side of the beach is slightly more shallow and crowded and the far right side is more peaceful.

Really enjoyed the snorkeling along the rocky side of the bay. We saw many species of fish. The morning was lovely but by the afternoon on a Friday the beach became extremely busy. I recommend you visit on any other week day.
Great beach  and you dont need snorkeling gear with just some goggles your good since the fishes are really close to the ocean shore
Beautiful day at Maguey. Mahi Mahi restaurant was great and a big thank you to the staff and especially Tonita.
Beautiful, quiet, warm water, lots of food to choose from,mostly seafood and there is no fee or wait time to enter the beach.

It is a beautiful beach but crowded and I dont enjoy when people is from the beginning offering things for sale all the time. Food is really expensive but you can bring your own but still need to buy at least a beer to be able to use the shade and the parking if you are driving.

Once you arrive, you will be approached immediately by restaurant staff trying to convince you to go into their restaurants. Facing the beach, walk to the right look for the bathrooms  showers  take the public path to the beach. The water was calm, lovely  incredibly clear. Be aware that in the afternoon numerous boat tours show up quickly and their customers are herded into the restaurants to be fleeced. I simply walked to a quiet area of the beach  set up my space and had an enjoyable time.
Beautifulonly thing this beach goes in deep very soon. Also be aware of the restaurants on the beach they tend to add extras to your tab.

Tons of beach side restaurants of which the staff will accost you as soon as you get out of a taxi, trying to get you to come to their place. At the far left end of the beach, theres a small public swimming area where we were able to snorkel from and see a wide variety of small fish. The waves and current there were fine for swimming and our kids being in the water. Beautiful turquoise, warm water.

comentario: loved this cathedral was built in the 1600s must see church in town loved the architecture and the wooden stages of the cross on the walls plus theres a special prayer room in the back

by todays standards this is a small church but when it was built im sure it was considered huge there are some old stained glass windows the doors are probably original to the structure and are enormous which begs the question if where they came from since la paz is desert parishioners were praying im not catholic so we didnt spend any real time in the church since its just 2 blocks from the waterfront it was worth the walk thoughts of being in a building dating from 1861 in such wonderful condition is amazing obviously services are still held and i remember reading a cornerstone mentioning a much earlier date in the 1700s a quick review of history dates la paz to 1535 so a town with a 500 year history in north america is another pretty good reason to visit one of its oldest structures

comentario: a must to visit in la paz beautiful with a warm flair inside you are allowed to go in whenever you want in the middle of the center close by the malecon boulevard

The Malecón in La Paz, Baja California Sur, is a 5kilometer stretch where you can walk, jog, or ride your bike. Its incredibly picturesque, with charming shops where you can buy souvenirs or simply enjoy the music played by musicians near the pier. Families stroll together, creating an atmosphere that feels like a dream neighborhood. Youll find restaurants to suit all budgets, offering some of the best seafood, including tuna, yellowfin, sea bass, or jurel. Its the perfect spot for a sunset dinner. We fell in love with this city it makes you feel safe, welcome, and well taken care of. The service is phenomenal. It was a truly special time that well always cherish in our memories. We had a great experience until we had been making our way out of La Paz back to Los Cabos. 1. The police will target foreigners to extort them for money. Usually these corrupt cops will make up something to stop you. For example I was stopped for speeding 90km in a 30km zone. I know I didnt, but hey if you argue they will arrest you and continue to extort you at the police station. I want to provide some tips for those that are traveling to Mexico and not fully staying a resort or have to drive as the corruption with police is anything like I have ever seen or experienced. 1. Be calm, respectful, but stern. 2. They will try to provoke, humiliate, or slightly threaten you. 3. Always try to stop in a well lit area 4. Carry 2 wallets and about 75100 for the eventual extortion you will need to pay 5. Dont go to La Paz unless you book a tour because it will cost you if you are likely stopped by the police. Dont try to pay them off immediately you might actually be dealing with a good cop and you are actually in the wrong. There are good cops... so be careful you dont get into more trouble for this. Usually you will get the hint this cop is corrupt. Contact your embassy if arrested. Trust me let them know you have been arrested and request assistance If it gets to this point. Corruption has its limits.

La Paz is the capital of the state, and Im not sure if that is the reason, but there are plenty of officers, and has added to the feeling of safety. Really interesting place to check out. The water is exceedingly shallow for quite a ways out, which allows you to see cracks and fish easily. There were plenty of people lounging in the water and along the shore. Important to note they recently added an entrance fee that must be paid online. There is no service within 1520 mins of the entrance so make sure you buy tickets ahead of time unlike me and my friends. They also have a cap on the number of people who can enter in the morning and then in the afternoon. They also require everyone to leave their morning or afternoon sessions at a set time, and blast a loud horn to signal its time to go. Glad we took the time to visit

Its okay. Water does not even come to knee level. Very smooth to walk in. Very pretty spot though not as nice or as pretty as the beach we visited on island espiritu santo.

We had an amazing experience at Playa Balandra. Important tip you must purchase your entry ticket digital bracelet in advance through the official CONANP websiteits required to access the beach. The process was easy and made everything very organized. We arrived around 100 PM and were already settled by 115super efficient. The controlled entry system really helps keep the beach peaceful and uncrowded. The area is absolutely stunningcalm, pristine, and incredibly well preserved. You can truly feel that its a protected natural reserve. Theres a visible presence of authorities, which makes everything feel safe and well managed. Everyone follows the rules, and the beach stays clean and serene. The water is crystal clear and shallow, almost like a natural poolhonestly one of the most magical beaches weve visited. Parking was accessible, and there are restrooms available, which is always a plus. Overall, a perfect experience. If youre visiting La Paz, this is a mustbut plan ahead and get your bracelet
Awsome beach

Balandra Beach was a true disappointment. Accessing it is unnecessarily complicated and not worth the effort. You need to purchase bracelets online about 15 per person, then drive to a designated supermarket to physically pick them up. After that, you still need to arrive early to secure entry during your assigned time slot. We arrived around 1230 pm for the afternoon entry and were able to get in fairly quickly, which initially seemed promising. However, once we reached the beach, the experience fell far short of expectations. The beach does not look nearly as impressive as it does in photos. The water is extremely shallow, and there is absolutely no shade. Most importantly, there are no amenitiesno chairs, umbrellas, or food options. As a visitor, youre expected to bring and carry everything yourself, which makes the whole experience inconvenient and uncomfortable. We left after just a few minutes and genuinely couldnt understand why this place is so heavily promoted as a mustsee in La Paz. Instead, we went to Tecolote Beach, which was a much better experience. It offers restaurants, chairs, and shaded areas for renteverything you need for an enjoyable and relaxing beach day. I wish there were more honest reviews about Balandrait would have saved us both time and money.
noun_phrases: an enjoyable and relaxing beach day

comentario: A gorgeous and unusual location, with warm, pristine blue water, surrounded by barren hills and teaming with marine life. A truly remarkable beach worth the drive.

But a nearby food truck was selling WiFi service for 150 pesos. Apparently this is a recent process change. After 30 minutes of online struggles we got our tickets and enjoyed the beach. It is a nice beach. It competes with many other nice beaches in the world.

I would NOT recommend this place to anyone. There is absolutely no clear information about needing a reservation in advance. When you arrive, after driving all the way there, they suddenly ask where your reservation was made and then casually explain that you were supposed to book through a specific platform or system. This information is not clearly communicated beforehand, and there are no proper signs or clear instructions. So you end up wasting your time, fuel, and energy driving there, only to be turned away and forced to go back. It feels disorganized and unprofessional. A place that operates like this clearly does not respect visitors time. Very disappointing experience. I would not return, and I would not recommend it to anyone.

Also many impressive birds flying the thermals above. Its amazing how many people arrive at the beach without supplies, creating a great business opportunity for the locals. As a protected beach they only allow a limited group in the morning, clear them out at noon, and another limited group in the afternoon, with closure at 5 pm.

We spent the whole day kayaking and exploring every little corner of this magical place. Be sure to book a guide so you can get a chance to see the mangroves. Those are not to be missed.

This was a really cool spot, but your experience will definitely depend on when you arrive. The day is split into two timeframes the first one runs until noon, and the second starts at 1 pm. Once the first session ends, everyone has to clear out, and a new group is let in an hour later. We had no problem getting into the morning session, but when we left, the line for the afternoon was already super long. And this was on a Tuesday morning I can only imagine how packed it must get on the weekends. The beach itself is peaceful and scenic, with calm, shallow water that only goes up to your knees, making it super safe for nonswimmers and perfect for families with kids. I was hoping for a good swim, but its not deep enough for that though honestly, it wasnt a dealbreaker. There are also a handful of palapas available for shade, but youll want to grab one early if you can. Its definitely worth checking out, but if youre aiming for the afternoon session, Id highly recommend arriving well ahead of time to beat the crowds.

Totally worth the drive from Cabo for an excellent day trip to this beautiful beach. Goes knee deep for a long stretch, and although we were warned about stingrays, we didnt see any. comentario: Gorgeous beach and a mustvisit if you are in the area Wading through it feels like being in a vast infinity pool of waisthigh cool turquoise waters, white sands and shoals of fishes swimming by. Arrive early at 7am for the 8am12pm slot and get a digital pass in advance because they dont allow more than 400 people in at a time. Come prepared with food and water as there wont be any on location. Its very hot, so either get your own umbrella or rent one on location and wear lots of sunscreen and a rashguard to avoid sunburn. Bathroom facilities exist but its a portable sort and not clean enough  I would have removed one star for it just for the state of the bathrooms if the venue wasnt as breathtakingly beautiful . Remember to shuffle through the sands as you wade because there might be sting rays underneath

We did enjoy the place at the early stage but when it become too crowded, too much music and noise, the magic went away.
Must visit  Something which is very rare.

comentario: Best beach in the world

I dont usually write reviews but Im hoping this can help someone who might be similarly confused about the new online ticketing system. In March, we were told to purchase the digital bracelets for entry online. We could not select which time slot we wanted, but we were able to use them to enter the beach during the morning session from 8am. On a Monday, we arrived at 7.45am to about 10 cars in front of us. As soon as it turned 8am we all moved in very quickly, showing the digital pdf on my phone to the staff, and it was an easy process. After reading reviews we were expecting a bit of a hectic tourist trap but it was very relaxed and it didnt feel at all crowded. The tide went out quickly and it was probably possible to walk the entire way to the other side of the bay before noon. Super shallow, super child friendly. We rented an umbrella and two chairs from a vendor while lined up at the entrance for 350 pesos there werent any inside, which gave us the freedom to sit anywhere we wanted on the beach, would recommend. The kayaks werent available for rent when we were there, so if youre super keen on that it might be worthwhile booking a tour.
Some of the clearest ocean water I have ever seen in my life. Very tranquil.
Amazing Try to get there early as its getting more and more popular and they limit how many ppl per day are allowed in

We visited on a Monday and left around 4am from Cabo San Lucas and arrived first in line at 7am. They limit the number of people who enter the beach so we made sure to get there super early but the beach never got too crowded. Entrance was free but they do charge to use the bathrooms so make sure to bring some cash. They have permanent umbrellas at the beach that you can use but there is also a gentleman at the entrance that rents out chairsumbrellas. They take your ID as collateral and will return it once you return the chairs back. They also had kayak and stand up paddle boards to rent. But no food vendors so make sure to come prepared Beautiful beach and very low tides. The hike up the hill was super easy we hiked in flip flops and the views were totally worth it. This was my first time visiting this beach and I will definitely be back
noun_phrases: Beautiful beach and very low tides

comentario: The water is spectacularly clear, but a word of caution, theres no place to buy water or food once inside the park

Arrived at 4 PM. Was told they are full for the day. Guard wouldnt let us in, even though there were cars leaving. Guard said we had to be there at 7 AM to get in at 8, or at 1130 to get in at 1 PM. Seems like a big effort and a long time to wait to go to the beach.
Playa Balandra is a place where the sea meets the desert. This beach offers a stunning and unique landscape. Its the best beach to relax and enjoy the natural beauty of La Paz, BCS
One of the best beaches I have ever been

Beautiful white sandy beach. If you arrive at low tide there wont be enough water for swimming but its still beautiful. They allow morning and afternoon shifts of people so it doesnt get too overcrowded. The views from the short hike near the playa are fabulous.
Beautiful beach, a lot of space for people to spread out. If youre coming from La Paz on the playa bus, take the noon bus and you will get in for the 1pm opening.
They limit the number of of cars to enter so make sure to arrive early
It is a beautiful beach, but maybe not the best beach in Mexico as they claim.

Heaven on earth You can walk in the water for miles and the water still wont reach your hips.

Such a gong show. Only limited people allowed and you drive all the way there and theyll turn you back after waiting in line. And its 500pesos per person. Also when the tide is low it looks like crap in some places. Boat entry is better. Once you take in the view not a lot there but shallow water. Place is worth seeing once otherwise meh. The view and the water was good, but I feel limiting the number of people is a marketing ploy, and after seeing the bay, I would say, if it wasnt for the marketing and limiting the number of people in, I probably wouldnt have gone out of my way to visit. But, I did, so it works. It wasnt a total waste of time, and I probably would have enjoyed it more if it wasnt built up so much.

Absolutely beautiful and unique. Bring food, as no cafes there. Amazing how far you can walk out in the shallow water. Came in the Winter midmorning when it was still a little windy and chilly. Warmed up by 11am but then they close and kick you out 121pm cant even do the short hike to see view during that time. People start lining up for entry before noon for afternoon session. Would recommend coming by 113012pm, line up and then you have longer to spend on the beach in the afternoon and weather is better. Andor go next door to Playa Tecolote. No line, beautiful views, bigger, parking right next to beach, cafes, hammocks, not quite as shallow. Best setup of cafehammocksbeach is all the way to the left. Either beach are a must see and spend halfday or all day
Wonderful beach with interesting geological structures. Tide was out when we were there so we go to see much more of the area.

Crystal clear water. White sands. The authority limits the amount of visitors so that the beach is never too crowded. Be aware that you may have to Wait in a queue before you can actually reach the beach. It is a great foto spot and worth the drive from la Paz
BEAUTIFUL beach LOVED this beach. I could spend hours out there. Just LOVE the colors and the wildlife.

Gorgeous perfect for little kids. As others have stated, pack in alot of your needed items towels, umbrella etc. We went to the morning slot they come do a sweep and clean up any garbage left behind please do your part but we didnt feel rushed out and it was slow so you couldve gotten back in the line to reenter. We had to leave at 1145 and the next session was let in at 1pm. It was def the slow season early December temperature was perfect and alot less people.
Great Beach for kids to explore...

Since Balandra is a protected area, keep in mind theres a limited capacity, so its essential to arrive early. You can get a spot either at 800 am or 100 pm. Unfortunately, they close at 500 pm, so catching the sunset there is hard. Its also important to note that phone reception is hard to find, so plan accordingly. Remember, there are no stores around, so its important to bring your own snacks and drinks. Also, pack an umbrella or chairs because there arent any available for rent onsite.

The is the rightfully famous, beautiful beach near La Paz.This spot has a timed entry policy in place. There is a AM slot that runs till midday and a PM slot that runs the afternoon. We arrived mid morning and went directly to gate attendant and he allowed us to go in to use the remaining time left for someone who had left early. We had about an hour at the beach, which was perfect. We then went next door Tecolote beach, which has restaurant service and no time limit.
This beach is a rare beauty. Highly recommend checking it out if you are in La Paz. There is so much wildlife so watch and it a cast area that is well protected by the conservation ministry.
Got to overview from the water on our boat tour, beautiful beach
Nice for a few hours or a day . Not really for snorkeling. Just to enjoy the incredible views

Balandra beach is a mustsee beach in La Paz. Warm, clear bluegreen, calm waters, you just may never want to get out. The water is shallow enough you can walk across the other side of the bay. Balandra has limited palapas 1520, recommend you bring your own umbrellas, chairs or blankie. You just have to find the right spot for you, on the beach. While youre at the beach, make sure you check out the famous mushroom rock, approximately a 15 minute walk or swim through shallow waters. Recommend water shoes. Traveling group and I visited in September. We arrived at the entrance gate and were told that beach was full to come back the following morning. We ended up going to neighboring beach, Playa Tecolote since we were in the area. Balandra has limited parking lot. No food vendors or restaurants. Bring your own food and drinks. Still recommend Playa Balandra

The gate security did not let me enter and I arrived at 4 pm. I told him I came from far away and he didnt care. I dont recommend wasting your day driving all the way here and they dont let you in. According to the gate person they close at 5 pm, but wouldnt let us in.
Two different times to go. I recommend the earlier time 812. After about 2 the water doesnt have that same wow factor. Rented a umbrella for 130 pesos.
Whatch out with the jellyfishes

Scenery wise, this beach is a 5  it really is a spectacular view with the beach nestled between the mountains. Swimming wise, a 0. I read the reviews before coming and there were a number of posts that pointed out the jellyfish medusas and aguamalas. This was no exaggeration. Taking precaution, I took 2 steps into the water and was trying to look around for jellyfish. Within 5 mins, I got stung. This species apparently is super invisible and swim around in groups. Its impossible to see to avoid. Luckily the beach staff had some vinegar spray which helped with the swelling, but theres no way I would go back into that water. Especially for children, I would avoid going into the water.

One of the most beautiful beaches I ve ever visited Organized, two shifts to enjoy it, 812 and 15. We went on a Saturday afternoon shift and it was ok, not full nor empty. Such a wonder of the world Magnificent shallow white sand beach in LA Paz. This beach is protected so there arent any shops. Just a beach and a parking lot. There are no garbage cans around so pack out what you brought in. Bathroom is kinda sus so I dont reccomend using it. Goodluck if you need to 2. The mangrove and dunes areas are off limits. Use only the one ans only trail to hike up for the views. The other trails are off limits as they arent trails and are dangerous. There are park Rangers at the park, I reccomend booking in advance. Limited amount of people allowed in, in 2 time slots of 81 and 15p. I recomend bringing umbrellas chairs. There are little umbrellas in the beach but they are first come first serve. You can also find tour groups that will take you to the beach but for a limited time.
Endless clean shallow waters.

Super nice chilled spot. They open at 1pm. You must get there like at 1130 to make sure you get a spot to get in. Bring everything you need. They really dont have anything to purchase. Bathrooms are outhouse and you have to tip to use the restrooms. Beach is awesome well worth. And stay to visit La Paz town.

Better line up in advance, but its worth it One of the most beautiful beaches in existence. Do NOT take pictures with the mushroom stone, will explain later I recommend you go during the morning slot, as the water levels are extremely low during the afternoon. The water is shallow inside the bay, I recommend you bring water shoes.

Felt like a pilgrimage through the shallow waters to see the mushroom rock. Beautifully clear water, and the fish are all harmless. We arrived after 845am on a Monday and got in just fine. I wish we would have rented one of the umbrellas from the vendors outside of the entrance, I recommend that to anyone listening as you will be in direct sun on the beach.
Great place even in the overcast day.

Playa Balandra was as beautiful as expected. We chose the afternoon time slot and arrived around 1130 AM, finding about 15 cars already in line. While waiting, we rented umbrellas and chairs from a vendor, knowing we wouldnt get one of the free palapas with so many cars and a bus ahead of us. Unfortunately, the chairs were the type that only allow you to sit straight up, not the reclining beach chairs we had hoped for. An umbrella is essential at this beach. People were let in around noon, and we found plenty of parking. The water was stunning, though the color changes as the afternoon progresses due to the way the sun reflects off the ocean. The water was most beautiful during the first couple of hours and started to look more like an ordinary ocean after 2 PM. I can see why the morning slot might be more appealing. I highly recommend the hike to the Mirador. The panoramic views from the top were breathtaking. It was an easy hike, taking only about five minutes from the bottom. If you pack lightly, you can secure a more secluded spot on the other side of the Mirador, which is challenging to reach if you have a lot of gear. This area is on the way to the mushroom rock.

Beautiful beach. We arrived at 11 am not knowing that there were two time frames that they let people in. The guy at the front gate just said it was closed until 1 So we asked a tour guide at the lower entrance what the situation was. He told us to get in the line at the bottom of the lot below. He said that the line gets long. So we got in line. Ended up being second in the line. We talked to the car infront of us and they gave us a bit more information on how getting into the beach works. They ended up opening the gate to us at about 1230. They let us know that the beach closes at 5. We parked found a palm umbrella to set up at and enjoyed the beach
One of my favorite places, the water is calm, nice and warm, somewhat shallow, beautiful place to beLa Paz baja CaliforniaMexico side

No place on earth like this. Only let a certain amount of people in for 4 hour time slots. 812 and 15. Not many shaded options so get there early to get one. Knee deep water all around with Mountain Views it is truly one of a kind. Must see if you are in La Paz. Shuffle your feet so you do not step on Rays
Went in the afternoon. Great parking right next to beach. The beach and water were wonderful. So expansive and low key. Everyone was friendly
The Worlds Aquarium

Yep, its as nice as everyone says it is. We took the bus there and to only complaint was that we didnt have very much time before the morning close at noon. Still, the white sand beach and shallow water in the Cove was so pretty. Look forward to going back again.

This is a very unique beach. The sand is white and the water is comfortable. You can walk completely across without getting submerged in the water. To get in you have to arrive about an hour and a half and wait in line until the gate opens, either at 9 or 1. This is an update to my previous post. We went to Balandra at the end of April 2024 at 1 pm. We didnt have to wait long to enter the beach, although there was a small line to get in. It was a great day and not too hot. Bring an umbrella, although they sell them at the entrance. People would place their umbrella into the water and sit in the water to enjoy the day. The sand is white and you can hike over to mushroom rock where the tour boats swing by. There are usually yachts sitting out to enjoy the beach. Its a wonderful beach. You shouldnt miss it when you visit La Paz.

It was one of the most beautiful beaches I have been to. Arrive early, for 8 to 12 slot.

Great beach with very shallow waters allowing you to walk to the middle of the sea
Get there early in the morning

Wow, Playa Balandra in La Paz, Baja California Sur is absolutely breathtaking Ive never seen such stunning scenery anywhere else. But I gotta admit, getting there isnt the easiest, mainly because the locals arent always clear on how things work, or maybe theyre just keeping it quiet not sure. Heres what I found out from my visit in March 2024 1. Youll need your own wheels to get to the beach. Taxis can take you there, but getting back might be tricky unless youve arranged something with the driver. 2. Since its a protected area, they only let in 400 people during two time slots early morning from around 78 am until noon, and then from 1 pm to 4 pm. But be warned, theres usually a long line of cars before each slot, so its best to arrive early to secure your spot. 3. And heres the kicker there are no facilities at the beach, so make sure to bring your own drinks and snacks. Plus, remember to take all your trash with you when you leave. 4. While youre waiting, you can rent chairs and umbrellas from vendors, although I didnt bother. 5. There are only a couple of portapotties near the parking lot, so plan accordingly. 6. Oh, and dont count on any cell service while youre there. Despite the hassle, the natural beauty and peaceful atmosphere of Playa Balandra make it totally worth the trip
noun_phrases: the natural beauty and peaceful atmosphere

comentario: One of the best beaches Ive seen, shallow water, great for the family, also the beach has two shifts to keep it from being packed, you also have to pay a fee to enter.
Beautiful beach that limits the number of people visiting so it never feels too crowded
Love it. Definitely going back.

A unique beach. Worth a days visit. It is protected so there is no food or drinks stuff you can buy at the beach. So bring your grub. You can kayak or walk for kilometers. Bring camara. There is a rock ridge if you want to avoid the sun. There is plenty and safe parking.
Beautiful blue water. You can wade the whole cove. Warmer water. Saw a sea turtle. Bathrooms are donation based. Dry toilets. Suggest doing your business before you arrive.

YOU MUST VISIT Its absolutely gorgeous the sand is so soft and the water is clear and warm. They have two visiting windows 812 and 15. Afternoon is packed and there is a long line to get in. There is no cost but you should bring everything you need with you cooler, umbrella, chair there are bathrooms for a few pesos which was wonderful
What a jewel of Mother Nature .. a Must visit if youre in these parts.

Beautiful hidden gem in the desert. Its where youll find the rock formation known as el hongo de la Paz. Its a protected beach. Therefore, plan accordingly since they have scheduled hours at this beach. It really is magical.
A must see place in La Paz. Beautiful beaches with breathtaking areas throughout from parking all the way to the arches and mushroom rock. Cant believe we walked so much but well worth the trip.
Very recomended there you can find yourself as well, is very peaceful and the ocean is very calm and relaxing, very very recomended

Beautiful and unique beach. Very shallow. You can walk out a far distance with the water only to your knees. We arrived at 1245pm on a Friday and we got in just fine. You have to pay a small tip to use the bathroom. The beach is clean. Please bring your own umbrellas, water, food etc. because nothing is there besides sand, rock and water which is gorgeous.

Very beautiful beach as everyone said but some logistics. There are two time slots to enter, 812 pm, and 15pm. Only 400 people can enter each time slot. We went for the 1 pm. We got there around 1145 am. Line can start forming around 12 pm, so I think getting there one hour is as early as you need to go. Since we were close to front of the line, we got to start going in the beach around 1215 pm. So even though the official start time in the afternoon was 1 pm, we still got to go in early. I personally think we wouldve got in anyways even if we arrived at 1 pm based on the crowd. Not sure if it ever got full afterwards, but reading reviews, it can get full where people get turned away. When you are lining up, there will be lots of people trying to sell you beach chairumbrellas. Note that the line forms at where all the people selling the umbrellas are. To enter the line, you go through a dirt road off a highway pass near the main road which someone will guide you to, even tho it feels wrong. We thought they were just try to get us to line up for the vendors. But it ended up being the main line. At the beach, theres bathroom but you need to pay. Therere no vendors besides kayaks for rent. Theres no entrance fee. There are around 20 permanent umbrellas you can use if you are one of the first ones to get in, otherwise bring your own.
Absolutely gorgeous. Maybe the nicest beach in all of Mexico.

The bomb Nothing like it This is a must see before you die Im not kidding Checked list ready

Beautiful beach, no doubt. But theres no information about the stingray present. I got bit by a stingray. Experienced the worst pain of my life. I got bit in the middle of the water, my husband had to carry my all the way to the parking. The staff did not help to bring me. They didnt have any antitode for the poison. They squeezed the poison out from an excruciating, painful wound. We had to drive 40 minutes to get to the first doctor.I felt like I was going to die. There were people and kids in the water. I can only imagine if a kid got bit, what would happen. There should be warning signs everywhere. I would recommend everyone to be very careful in the water

Playa Balandra is one of the best beach in Mexico so far I have visited. It is one of the famous and highly recommended beach for tourists. But till now none of the reviews have honest and clear information about how and when in a day to visit. They have 2 slots in a day i,e 1st slot is 8am to 12am and 2nd slot is 1 pm to 5pm. And its first come first serve. No tickets , no pre booking. So if your are planning to visit at morning slots then you have to be there at least 5am and again hall depends on luck. If 400 people are already in queue before you then no hopes for that slot and if you have patience for 2nd slot, then will have to wait till 12. And there will be queue just 300 meter from the security checkpoint. And its a car queue.. and the security will come and give a token for headcount. But it was worth waiting . So if you and you family planning to enjoy this beautiful beach , please plan ahead accordingly. Inside no vendors, so you have to carry each and everything.. hope this helps
noun_phrases: the famous and highly recommended beach

comentario: Absolutely gorgeous beach. The water is amazing, the views are stunning. There are no services or vendors here, so come prepared.

Playa Balandra in Mexico and it was absolutely breathtaking In my opinion, its one of the most stunning beaches in the country. Its even comparable to Isla Mujeres, which is saying a lot The crystalclear turquoise waters and white sand beaches are truly a sight to behold. I cant wait to go back and soak up the sun again.

The most beautiful beach. We went with a private tour, driven to the beach and set up umbrella, lunch etc. You could easily do this alone, we saw taxis dropping people off at the entrance and walking in. The beach has morning slots from 812, then closes 121 and reopens 15pm. Recommend getting up early as their queue to get in. We arrived for 0730am and waited in queue till 0800am to get in. They only allow a certain number of people on the beach, this number does change, on the day we went it was 450 people allowed for the morning slot. Would recommend going in the morning as there is very limited shade umbrellas bring own food and drink non on sight. Public toilets for a small fee available also. A short hike up a steep hill for the most incredible views too.

Arrive early if you want a great spot. We arrived at 0645 and were pretty deep in the line already. Make sure you bring your cooler and some cold drinks. Otherwise it was a great experience. Lots of puffer fish and small schools of other fish. My wife and I really enjoyed our time there.

A beautiful Federally Protected Bay. Visitors are limited to around 400450 at a time. There are 2 shifts for visitors, an 8am12pm and a 1pm5pm. Get there early to start lining up before gates open. Lots of puffer fish and you may see some sting rays. You can rent kayaks and there are street vendors who will rent you beach umbrellas before you enter the bay. There are a few palapas, which are first comefirst served.

This is perhaps the most beautiful beach in the world and wellhidden. Please disregard the commercial website links that scam you into buying a pass for this free park. You do not need to prepurchase a bracelet and pick it up from their store to get into a public park. Just follow the local Mexican adventurers and tourists who are probably just as disgusted with the scams.  As of June 2023, there are currently two sessions to limit the amount of people allowed in at one time. The first session is between 8 AM and 12 PM. The second is between 1 PM and 5 PM. Park rangers will manage traffic at the gate, but it was no problem to get in on a weekday. Parking is free and there is an unpaved lot with many spots. Overflow parking is available, but there is a medium walk in. Some locals choose to use a taxi. The beach has soft, white sand and you can walk all the way across because the water is chestdeep at most. Kayaks are available for rental. There are public restrooms. There are no food or drink vendors. Take out when you pack in and keep this beach beautiful
Amazing beaches and controlled access. There are two turns 8am12pm and 1pm5pm. Get there and get in line early

Playa Balandra lived up to the hype. The views from the mirador small hike after entering are worth the trip alone. Two time slots 8am 12pm then they clear out the beach. Second slot is 1pm 5pm. I got there at 730am and was first five cars in line. Got in exactly at 8 no problem. But the line when we left at 12pm for the 1pm was crazy long, so I would recommend coming at the 8am slot if you dont want to risk it getting full. Other things to note, They have 4 beach dogs that are harmless. There are a lot of rocks so I suggest you bring water shoes. There is no entrance fee, but there is a small charge to use the bathroom. And lastly, the rock does not look like a mushroom, but youll take a picture with it anyway.

Best beach I have ever been to. There are two shifts where they let people in. From 800 am to 1100 and from 1200 to 300. A limited number of cars are allowed in. The water is amazing. Cool and not deep at all. Great for kids.

Tried driving up from La Paz on Thursday afternoon but couldnt get in dt max occupancy. On Friday we showed up at 730 am and waited in the car until 8 for the beach to open. We were maybe the 10th car lined up, got in no problem limited to 400 people per day. No entry fee. We were told we could stay until noon. Easy, close parking to the permanent beach umbrellas. No services except a little row of portapotties, for which you have to pay 5 pesos theyll provide toilet paper. The beach was pretty but we got bad weather cold, cloudy, and very windy which really limited visibility through the water and was just annoying getting into everyones eyes and mouth. Bring a jacket or, better yet, a wetsuit just in case A lot of people with only beach clothes looked miserable. Did see a stingray I think Again, poor visibility and a couple schools of small fish while wading around, I was careful to drag my feet so as not to directly step on any wildlife. If I could do it again I would opt for the afternoon timeslot instead, higher chance of good weather. Big line of people to get a picture with the mushroom.

One of the most beautiful beaches I have ever seen in my entire life Its a true masterpiece of nature, which changes completely from morning, with low tide, to evening, with high tide. The parking is convenient, but I recommend going early because access to the beach is fortunately limited. Bring water with you because there are no bars It is well maintained and monitored by the Mexican government to preserve it as best as possible. The only disappointment was seeing so much plastic deposited along the roads to reach it, such as the road to Baja California Sur La Paz El Tecolote, which is a real openair dump. With the winds, there is a danger that it could also pollute the precious Sea of Cortez, so I hope that local organizations and the Mexican government can clean it up as soon as possible. I hope that this wonderful and unique nature can be preserved forever and attract tourists who are attentive and respectful of the place.

comentario: Solid beach on the edge of town that somehow works for both family vibes and cracking beers with the bros. Plenty of free palapas to claim, or you can spring for an umbrella setup with chairs and a table. Food is available tootake the short walk up to the restaurants or kick back and let tableside service come to you. The only buzzkill Shards of broken glass in the sand. I didnt hit anything sharp, but bottles on the beach are a trash movestick to cans. If people showed this place a little more respect, itd easily be a 5star recommendation. As it stands, the vibe is there, the setup is there, but the glass is a safety hazard.

Its an excellent choice if youre looking for a convenient, familyfriendly beach close to the city. The beach is wellmaintained and has all the essentials you need for a comfortable day by the water. One of the biggest perks is its accessibility. Its a quick and easy drive from downtown La Paz, and theres plenty of parking available. You dont have to worry about a long journey to find a great spot. The water is typically calm and shallow, which makes it perfect for kids to swim and play in. There are also palapas and lounge chairs you can rent, so youre not stuck laying a towel directly on the sand. The onsite restaurant and food stalls are a huge plus, offering a decent variety of food and drinks. The bathrooms are also clean and wellkept. The only reason Im not giving it five stars is that it can get pretty crowded, especially on weekends and holidays. The atmosphere is more busy public beach than tranquil escape. If youre looking for a quiet, secluded spot, this might not be it. However, if youre traveling with family or want a beach with amenities and a lively vibe, its a fantastic option. Its a great place to spend a fun, hasslefree day soaking up the sun and enjoying the Sea of Cortez.
